Items.Title: 在 Unity3D 中创建和导入自定义 android 库 | Create and import a custom android library in Unity3D
Items.Description:
在某些情况下，我们需要使用 Unity3D 中的本机库访问设备或 API。 当涉及到硬件 API 调用时，例如电池百分比或 android 版本上的 CPU 使用率，在 Unity3D 上没有直接访问这些的方法。

在本节中，我们将创建一个 .aar 格式的原生 android 插件并将其集成到 Unity3D 应用程序中。

入门

我们可以使用资产商店和统一包来为我们的统一项目实现附加功能。 但是，在某些情况下，没有可用于原生 android 模块的统一包或资产商店资产。 我们可以通过使用 Android studio 构建一个原生的 android 插件来解决这个问题。

但是，Android 库会编译成 Android 存档 (AAR) 文件，该文件可用作项目的依赖项。

让我们开始创建我们的原生插件。

要求

一、Android项目设置

  • 打开安卓工作室。 单击新建项目按钮以创建一个 android 项目。 选择空活动。 在创建新项目窗口中提供必要的信息，然后单击完成按钮以创建项目。



  • 在 gradle build 之后，右键单击 app 文件夹，然后选择 New → Module 选项以创建一个新的 Android 模块。 选择“Android 库”模板。 将其余信息保留为默认值（如果需要，您可以更改它）。 单击完成按钮以创建一个新的 Android 模块。

定义您的自定义库

我们可以定义一个自定义库来使用 Toast 方法在 UI 中显示电池电量信息。 让我们从我们的原生插件开始打印从 Unity3D 传递的字符串。

  • 右键单击模块→ java 文件夹。 选择 New → Java Class 并提供一个有效的名称（在本例中，我们将使用 Alert）。
  • 将以下代码添加到警报类。
 package com.codemaker.mylibrary; import android.app.Application; import android.content.Context; import android.os.Handler; import android.os.Looper; import android.widget.Toast; public class Alert extends Application { // our native method, which will be called from Unity3D public void PrintString(Context context, final String message) { //create / show an android toast, with message and short duration. new Handler(Looper.getMainLooper()).post(new Runnable() { @Override public void run() { Toast.makeText(context, message, Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show(); } }); } }

同样，我们可以再创建一个名为BatteryLevelIndicator的类来提供电池电量信息。

  • 将以下代码添加到BatteryLevelIndicator类。
    •  package com.codemaker.mylibrary; import android.app.Application; import android.content.Context; import android.content.Intent; import android.content.IntentFilter; import android.os.BatteryManager; public class BatteryLevelIndicator extends Application { // Return the battery level as a float between 0 and 1 // (1 being fully charged, 0 fulled discharged) public float GetBatteryPct(Context context) { Intent batteryStatus = GetBatteryStatusIntent(context); int level = batteryStatus.getIntExtra(BatteryManager.EXTRA_LEVEL, -1); int scale = batteryStatus.getIntExtra(BatteryManager.EXTRA_SCALE, -1); float batteryPct = level / (float)scale; return batteryPct; } // Return whether or not we're currently on charge public boolean IsBatteryCharging(Context context) { Intent batteryStatus = GetBatteryStatusIntent(context); int status = batteryStatus.getIntExtra(BatteryManager.EXTRA_STATUS, -1); return status == BatteryManager.BATTERY_STATUS_CHARGING || status == BatteryManager.BATTERY_STATUS_FULL; } private Intent GetBatteryStatusIntent(Context context) { IntentFilter ifilter = new IntentFilter(Intent.ACTION_BATTERY_CHANGED); return context.registerReceiver(null, ifilter); } }

    构建原生库

    按工具栏中的“Make Project”按钮 (CTRL + F9) 来构建 Android 模块。

    编译后，您会在app\<library-name>\build\outputs\aar文件夹下找到一个 android 归档文件。

    使用 Unity Hub 创建一个新的 Unity3D 项目。
  • 打开您的 Unity3D 项目并创建文件夹结构：Assets / Plugins / Android 并将自定义库 ( mylibrary-debug.aar ) 放入文件夹中。

    • 本机库集成

    • 创建一个 C# 脚本以从应用程序访问导入的库。 为此，创建一个名为 Scripts 的文件夹。 在 Scripts 文件夹中添加脚本并将其命名为 NativeCodeRunner.cs 。 将以下代码添加到其中。
     using System.Collections; using System.Collections.Generic; using UnityEngine; public class NativeCodeRunner : MonoBehaviour { void Start() { string msg = "Battery Level: " + (GetBatteryLevel() * 100) + "%"; ShowToast(msg); } //method that calls our native plugin. public void ShowToast(string msg) { if (Application.platform == RuntimePlatform.Android) { // Retrieve the UnityPlayer class. AndroidJavaClass unityPlayerClass = new AndroidJavaClass("com.unity3d.player.UnityPlayer"); // Retrieve the UnityPlayerActivity object AndroidJavaObject unityActivity = unityPlayerClass.GetStatic<AndroidJavaObject>("currentActivity"); // Retrieve the "Bridge" from our native plugin. // ! Notice we define the complete package name. AndroidJavaObject alert = new AndroidJavaObject("com.codemaker.mylibrary.Alert"); // Setup the parameters we want to send to our native plugin. object[] parameters = new object[2]; parameters[0] = unityActivity; parameters[1] = msg; // Call PrintString in bridge, with our parameters. alert.Call("PrintString", parameters); } } public float GetBatteryLevel() { if (Application.platform == RuntimePlatform.Android) { AndroidJavaClass unityPlayerClass = new AndroidJavaClass("com.unity3d.player.UnityPlayer"); AndroidJavaObject unityActivity = unityPlayerClass.GetStatic<AndroidJavaObject>("currentActivity"); AndroidJavaObject alert = new AndroidJavaObject("com.codemaker.mylibrary.BatteryLevelIndicator"); object[] parameters = new object[1]; parameters[0] = unityActivity; return alert.Call<float>("GetBatteryPct", parameters); } return -1f; } }
    • 创建一个 Empty GameObject 并将 NativeCodeRunner 脚本添加到其中。

    设置项目

    • 打开项目设置（文件→构建设置）并将目标平台切换到Android。
    • 通过单击 Add Open Scenes 按钮将当前场景添加到构建中。
    • 提供包名称并将最低目标版本更改为 Android 7.1 'Nougat'（API 级别 25）。
    • 最新的 android 版本对从另一个应用程序访问活动有一些安全限制。 如果 android 版本大于 30，则将 android:exported 设置为“true”以允许其他应用程序访问它。为此，请在 Player 设置下的 Publishing 设置中启用自定义主 Android 清单文件。
    • 从 Plugins/Android 文件夹中打开 AndroidManifest.xml 文件。 在 Activity 标签中添加 android:exported=”true” 代码并删除以下注释。
     <!-- GENERATED BY UNITY. REMOVE THIS COMMENT TO PREVENT OVERWRITING WHEN EXPORTING AGAIN-->

    最终的 AndroidManifest.xml 将如下所示，

     <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android" package="com.unity3d.player" xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools"> <application> <activity android:name="com.unity3d.player.UnityPlayerActivity" android:theme="@style/UnityThemeSelector" android:exported="true"> <intent-filter> <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" /> <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" /> </intent-filter> <meta-data android:name="unityplayer.UnityActivity" android:value="true" /> </activity> </application> </manifest>

    运行应用程序

    通过 USB 数据线连接移动设备，然后单击 Build and Run 按钮在目标设备上运行应用程序。 确保在您的设备上启用了 USB 调试。

    你有它！ 在 Unity 3D 中使用自定义原生 android 库的您自己的 android 应用程序 :)

    感谢您阅读本文。

    感谢 Gowri M Bhatt 审阅内容。

    如果您喜欢这篇文章，请点击拍手按钮👏并分享以帮助其他人找到它！

    这篇文章也可以在Medium上找到。

    本教程的完整源代码可以在这里找到，

    GitHub - codemaker2015/unity-android-native-library-demo unity-android-native-library-demo,下载unity-android-native-library-demo的源码_GitHub_帮酷



    原文:

    There are scenarios where we need to access devices or APIs using native libraries in Unity3D. When it comes to hardware API calls such as battery percentage or CPU usage on android builds, there is no direct way to access these on Unity3D.

    In this section, we will create a native android plugin in .aar format and integrate it into the Unity3D application.

    Getting Started

    We can use asset store and unity packages for implementing additional functionalities for our unity projects. However, there are scenarios where there are no unity package or asset store assets available for native android modules. We can solve the problem by building a native android plugin using Android studio.

    An Android library includes everything required to build an app, including source code, resource files, and an Android manifest. However, an Android library compiles into an Android Archive (AAR) file which can be used be as dependencies on projects.

    Let’s start creating our native plugin.

    Requirements

    A. Android project setup

    • Open Android Studio. Click on the new project button to create an android project. Select the Empty activity. Provide necessary information on the create new project window and click on the Finish button to create the project.



    • After the gradle build, right click on the app folder then select New → Module option to create a new Android module. Select the “Android Library” template. Keep the remaining information as defaults (you can alter it if required). Click on the Finish button to create a new Android module.

    Define your custom library

    We can define a custom library to display the battery level information in the UI using the Toast method. Let’s begin with our native plugin to print a string passed from Unity3D.

    • Right click on the module → java folder. Select New → Java Class and provide a valid name (In this example, we will use Alert).
    • Add the following code to the Alert class. 
    package com.codemaker.mylibrary;
import android.app.Application;
import android.content.Context;
import android.os.Handler;
import android.os.Looper;
import android.widget.Toast;
public class Alert extends Application {
    // our native method, which will be called from Unity3D
    public void PrintString(Context context, final String message) {
        //create / show an android toast, with message and short duration.
        new Handler(Looper.getMainLooper()).post(new Runnable() {
            @Override
            public void run() {
                Toast.makeText(context, message, Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
            }
        });
    }
}

    The Alert class has a PrintString() method which takes two arguments — context and string.

    • Similarly, we can create one more class named BatteryLevelIndicator to provide the battery level information.
    • Add the following code to the BatteryLevelIndicator class. 
    package com.codemaker.mylibrary;
import android.app.Application;
import android.content.Context;
import android.content.Intent;
import android.content.IntentFilter;
import android.os.BatteryManager;
public class BatteryLevelIndicator extends Application {
    // Return the battery level as a float between 0 and 1
    // (1 being fully charged, 0 fulled discharged)
    public float GetBatteryPct(Context context) {
        Intent batteryStatus = GetBatteryStatusIntent(context);
int level = batteryStatus.getIntExtra(BatteryManager.EXTRA_LEVEL, -1);
        int scale = batteryStatus.getIntExtra(BatteryManager.EXTRA_SCALE, -1);
float batteryPct = level / (float)scale;
        return batteryPct;
    }
// Return whether or not we're currently on charge
    public boolean IsBatteryCharging(Context context) {
        Intent batteryStatus = GetBatteryStatusIntent(context);
int status = batteryStatus.getIntExtra(BatteryManager.EXTRA_STATUS, -1);
        return status == BatteryManager.BATTERY_STATUS_CHARGING ||
                status == BatteryManager.BATTERY_STATUS_FULL;
    }
    private Intent GetBatteryStatusIntent(Context context) {
        IntentFilter ifilter = new IntentFilter(Intent.ACTION_BATTERY_CHANGED);
        return context.registerReceiver(null, ifilter);
    }
}

    Build native library

    Press the “Make Project” button ( CTRL + F9 ) in the toolbar to build the Android Module.

    After compilation, you will find an android archive file located under app\<library-name>\build\outputs\aar folder.

    B. Use the native plugin inside the Unity3D project

    • Create a new Unity3D project using Unity Hub.
    • Open your Unity3D project and create the folder structure: Assets / Plugins / Android and Place the custom library (mylibrary-debug.aar) inside the folder.

    Native library integration

    • Create a C# script to access the imported library from the app. For which, create a folder named Scripts . Add the script inside Scripts folder and name it NativeCodeRunner.cs . Add the following code to it. 
    using System.Collections;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using UnityEngine;
public class NativeCodeRunner : MonoBehaviour {
 void Start() {
  string msg = "Battery Level: " + (GetBatteryLevel() * 100) + "%";
  ShowToast(msg);
 }
 //method that calls our native plugin.
 public void ShowToast(string msg) {
  if (Application.platform == RuntimePlatform.Android) {
   // Retrieve the UnityPlayer class.
   AndroidJavaClass unityPlayerClass = new AndroidJavaClass("com.unity3d.player.UnityPlayer");
   // Retrieve the UnityPlayerActivity object 
   AndroidJavaObject unityActivity = unityPlayerClass.GetStatic<AndroidJavaObject>("currentActivity");
   // Retrieve the "Bridge" from our native plugin.
   // ! Notice we define the complete package name.              
   AndroidJavaObject alert = new AndroidJavaObject("com.codemaker.mylibrary.Alert");
   // Setup the parameters we want to send to our native plugin.              
   object[] parameters = new object[2];
   parameters[0] = unityActivity;
   parameters[1] = msg;
   // Call PrintString in bridge, with our parameters.
   alert.Call("PrintString", parameters);
  }
 }
 public float GetBatteryLevel() {
   if (Application.platform == RuntimePlatform.Android) {
       AndroidJavaClass unityPlayerClass = new AndroidJavaClass("com.unity3d.player.UnityPlayer");
       AndroidJavaObject unityActivity = unityPlayerClass.GetStatic<AndroidJavaObject>("currentActivity");        
       AndroidJavaObject alert = new AndroidJavaObject("com.codemaker.mylibrary.BatteryLevelIndicator");            
       object[] parameters = new object[1];
       parameters[0] = unityActivity;
       return alert.Call<float>("GetBatteryPct", parameters);
   }
   return -1f;
 }
}
    • Create an Empty GameObject and add the NativeCodeRunner script to it.

    Setup the project

    • Open project settings (File → Build settings) and switch the target platform to Android.
    • Add the current scene to the build by clicking on the Add Open Scenes button.
    • Provide a package name and change the Minimum target version to Android 7.1 ‘Nougat’ (API level 25).
    • The latest android version has some security restrictions to access the activity from another app. If the android version is greater than 30 then set android:exportedto “true" to allow other apps to access it. For which, enable the custom main Android manifest file from the Publishing settings under the Player settings.
    • Open the AndroidManifest.xml file from the Plugins/Android folder. Add android:exported=”true” code in the Activity tag and remove the following comment. 
    <!-- GENERATED BY UNITY. REMOVE THIS COMMENT TO PREVENT OVERWRITING WHEN EXPORTING AGAIN-->

    The final AndroidManifest.xml will look as the following,

    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest
    xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    package="com.unity3d.player"
    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools">
    <application>
        <activity 
            android:name="com.unity3d.player.UnityPlayerActivity"
            android:theme="@style/UnityThemeSelector"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <meta-data android:name="unityplayer.UnityActivity" android:value="true" />
        </activity>
    </application>
</manifest>

    Run the app

    Connect the mobile device via USB cable and click on the Build and Run button to run the app on the target device. Ensure that USB debugging is enabled on your device.

    There you have it! Your own android app with a custom native android library in Unity 3D :)

    Thanks for reading this article.

    Thanks Gowri M Bhatt for reviewing the content.

    If you enjoyed this article, please click on the clap button 👏 and share to help others find it!

    The article is also available on Medium.

    The full source code for this tutorial can be found here,

    GitHub - codemaker2015/unity-android-native-library-demo

Items.Title: 构建一个简单的 TikTok 抓取 API 🤖 | Building a simple TikTok scraping API 🤖
Items.Description:
作为以 TikTok 为中心的项目的一部分（即将推出！），我需要一种获取视频媒体的方法。 所以我需要另一种方法来检索这些信息。

利用 SSR 模式📦

作为一个处理大量流量的网站，我知道他们正在进行某种 SSR/客户端水合作用。 这通常涉及在<script>标记内内联的对象中向客户端发送数据，通过直接使用此数据填充组件来减少必要的客户端请求的数量。 例如，在Next.js中，这是通过服务器端 props发生的。

你瞧！ 每个视频的页面标记旁边都有一个方便的 blob。 您可以通过访问任何 TikTok 视频页面 ( https://www.tiktok.com/:user/video/:id ) 并通过控制台查看SIGI_STATE标签的内容来自己确认这一点：

 JSON . parse ( document . getElementById ( " SIGI_STATE " ). innerText );

知道了这一点，我使用deno构建了一个简单的处理程序来获取这个 blob 并直接返回它。

输入cheerio & deno 🥣🦕

如果视频 URL 附加到请求（通过POST正文或参数），一个简单的处理程序可以获取（抓取）页面的内容：

 const response = await fetch ( `https://tiktok.com/ ${ video } ` ); const html = await response . text ();

然后，使用cheerio ，这个原始文本可以变成一个可查询的类jQuery API。 这提供了一个入口点来获取脚本标记中服务器生成的 blob 的内容：

 const $ = cheerio . load ( html ); const appContext = $ ( " #SIGI_STATE " ). text ();

从那里开始，只需将其解析为 JSON 并在响应 ( ItemModule ) 中返回相关数据。 数据当前由一些唯一标识符键入， Object.keys有助于删除：

 const json = JSON . parse ( appContext ); const key = Object . keys ( json . ItemModule )[ 0 ]; const data = json . ItemModule [ key ]; return new Response ( JSON . stringify ({ ... data }), { headers : { ' Content-Type ' : ' application/json ' }, status : 200 , });

就是这样！ 现在我可以发出客户端请求来获取视频的最新媒体并直接显示它。 这也绕过了 TikTok 的 CDN 到期，这将阻止保存对该媒体的引用。

旁注：将其与我遇到的一个非官方 API 选项的输出进行比较，数据看起来相同。 所以很可能他们正在做同样的事情（但无限使用收费近 200 美元/月）！

无论如何，这很脆弱，如果他们决定重命名脚本标签、速率限制等，可能会中断。但这表明为您自己的项目启动实例是多么容易。 也许有了足够的机器人流量，TikTok 会创建一个公共 API 来完成此任务，而不是让我们为之努力！ 😇 😇



原文:

As part of a project centered around TikTok (coming soon!) I needed a way to get a video's media. The closest thing that TikTok officially provides is their oembed API, but unfortunately this only includes basic information like title, thumbnail_url etc. And other unofficial APIs cost money or require python, which I don't want to do. So I needed another way to retrieve this info.

Leveraging SSR patterns 📦

As a website that handles large volumes of traffic I knew they had some sort of SSR/client-side hydration going on. This generally involves is shipping data to the client in an object inlined inside a <script> tag, reducing the number of necessary client-side requests by directly populating components with this data. In Next.js for example, this happens via server-side props.

And lo and behold! There's a handy blob provided alongside the page markup for every video. You can confirm this for yourself by visiting any TikTok video's page (https://www.tiktok.com/:user/video/:id) and looking at the contents of the SIGI_STATE tag via console:

JSON.parse(document.getElementById("SIGI_STATE").innerText);

Knowing this I built a simple handler using deno to fetch this blob and return it directly.

Enter cheerio & deno 🥣🦕

If the video URL is attached to the request (via POST body or params), a simple handler can fetch (scrape) the content for the page:

const response = await fetch(`https://tiktok.com/${video}`);
const html = await response.text();

Then, using cheerio, this raw text can be turned into a queryable jQuery-like API. This provides an entrypoint to get the content of the server-generated blob within the script tag:

const $ = cheerio.load(html);
const appContext = $("#SIGI_STATE").text();

From there, it's only a matter of parsing this as JSON and returning pertinent data in the response (ItemModule). The data is currently keyed by some unique identifier, Object.keys helps remove that:

const json = JSON.parse(appContext);
const key = Object.keys(json.ItemModule)[0];
const data = json.ItemModule[key]; return new Response(JSON.stringify({ ...data }), { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, status: 200,
});

And that's it! Now I can make a client-side request to get up-to-date media for a video and display it directly. This also gets around TikTok's CDN expiration which would prevent saving a reference to this media.

Side-note: comparing this to the output for one of the unofficial API options I came across, the data looks identical. So it's likely they're doing the same thing (but charging almost $200/mo for unlimited usage)!

Anyways, this is fragile and could break if they decide to rename the script tag, rate-limit, etc. But this demonstrates how easy it is to spin up an instance for your own project. And maybe with enough bot traffic TikTok will create a public API that accomplishes this instead of making us scrape for it! 😇

Items.Title: 正确管理您的 Dynamics 365 Marketing 表单 | Manage your Dynamics 365 Marketing Forms properly
Items.Description:
由于营销工具（在线营销、电子邮件营销和实时营销）的复杂性以及 D365 营销模块在其产品生命周期中“年轻”这一事实，该营销自动化解决方案被认为比其他 Dynamics 365 受到更多批评更“成熟”的产品。

这是我在与客户和其他 D365 营销职能顾问合作时听到的最突出的批评之一：

可定制可能走得太远，非常参与开发所需的东西。 需要时间和资源的投入。 似乎没有人知道如何让它发挥作用，即使是所谓的专家。

这个痛点是非常有效的，因为我看到了许多实施、定制和管理不善的 D365 Marketing 实例。 在本文中，我将使用营销表单（用于获取潜在客户的最常用营销工具之一）来证明这一点，并演示如何为客户正确实施和维护 D365 营销表单以避免不必要的挫败感。

下一节中讨论的主题需要一些有关 D365 营销表格的知识

一般来说，营销形式需要遵循客户的品牌原则，以便它们可以无缝地嵌入到客户的面向公众的网站中。 在这种情况下，营销表格需要相应地设置样式，除非您的客户愿意使用看起来像是来自 1980 年网页的营销表格：

如果您使用过 D365 Marketing 提供的营销表单设计器，您可能知道或已经抱怨它缺乏样式可配置性。 是的，您可以配置表单的布局、字体大小、文本对齐方式、文本颜色和表单元素的填充，但这些功能几乎无法制作出一个近乎完美的 Web 表单。

即使您可以让倾斜的 reCAPTCHA 块和未设置样式的按钮飞起来，添加到营销表单的任何新字段都需要使用编辑器用户界面单独设置样式。 作为一名职能顾问，您可能希望您的时间最好花在其他地方。

幸运的是，表单设计器提供了一个 HTML 编辑器，可供 Web 开发人员（或任何对 HTML 和 CSS 有足够知识的人）使用，以根据客户的需要自定义 Web 表单外观。 然而，这种定制也可能给我们带来一些非常混乱的场景，这些场景通常会导致引用批评的第一部分——“可定制可能走得太远，因此解决方案需要时间和资源的投资”。

假设您 - 一名职能顾问 - 已经收集了客户的品牌需求，并使用表单设计器将所有字段添加到营销表单中。 但是，基本表单设计器无法实现营销表单的精美设计，因此任务被传递给开发团队中的前端开发人员，以便可以使用自定义 CSS 正确设置表单样式。 这似乎没什么大不了的，因为每个前端开发人员（即使是初级开发人员）都可以编写 CSS 并设置表单样式，对吧？

不幸的是，从解决方案设计的角度来看，这种实现非常令人担忧。

  • 如果您的开发团队中的开发人员没有遵循完全相同的强制编码模式和类命名原则，则此实现可能会导致所有可用营销表单的 HTML/CSS 代码库非常繁琐且混乱
  • 如果营销表单样式需要根据客户的反馈进行更新（这种情况非常频繁）并且任务分配给了另一个开发人员，他/她可能无法遵循之前的开发人员所做的工作，而只是继续添加他/她自己的 CSS 样式来覆盖以前的样式，这会导致更多不必要的代码行
  • 如果一个新的无样式字段被添加到现有表单中，或者如果有另一个类似的表单需要一些自定义样式，您和您的开发人员需要冲洗并重复这个痛苦的低效和混乱的过程。

如果我们沿着这条路走下去，我们将依靠一群人（来自各个级别）的勤奋来实施和维护一个营销形式。 此外，功能顾问（没有扎实的 HTML/CSS 经验）将始终依赖开发人员进行自定义样式更改。 再者，如果人家把代码库变成了一堆意大利面，技术支持根本无法排查问题，客户经常会说“没人知道怎么回事，所谓的专家也不知道”，

有什么更优雅的解决方案？ 利用表单模板和自定义属性为非技术 D365 Marketing 用户启用其他表单设计器选项。

有时我们确实批评 Microsoft 记录不完善的功能，但这不会是其中一种情况：自定义属性功能记录在MS Docs for Dynamics 365 Marketing 下。

为了总结实施过程，您的一位开发人员需要将所有常用的表单元素（文本字段、下拉字段、单选输入等）添加到营销表单模板并启用自定义配置属性，例如每个表单之间的间隙字段和输入标签的颜色。 这可以通过将元标记添加到表单模板的 HTML 并将相关的 CSS 属性更改为模板格式来完成。 如果您对设置的详细信息感兴趣，我将在未来发布指南。

那么这种实施有什么好处呢？

  • 表单字段的类型是明确的，客户端只需要对表单字段进行一定程度的控制（字段间距、按钮颜色、悬停时的按钮颜色等），所有这些都可以由开发人员在一个营销表单模板。 这意味着您所涉及的依赖项更少，因此客户端不会持续支持诸如更改按钮背景颜色之类的小事情。

  • 诚然，这种实现并没有完全消除 CSS 编码不佳的风险，但它确实通过让开发人员只实现代码库一次来减轻问题的严重性。

  • 自定义配置可以是通用的，因此您或客户可以通过仅修改一个字段来简单地更改所有输入标签的颜色或字体大小等。 样式更改也将应用于添加到营销表单的新字段。

  • 迁移到另一个环境的过程非常简单和简化。 只需复制营销表单模板的 HTML 并将其粘贴到在另一个环境中创建的新营销表单模板中，您就可以开始将模板用于新表单。

当您更改配置时，例如，使用颜色选择器更改标签文本颜色时，所有标签都会更改颜色。

这种实现使任何人（即使没有 HTML/CSS 知识）都可以更轻松地控制高级样式属性，从而无需开发人员的持续支持。 此外，如果客户向您抱怨某些配置不起作用，您知道它不会是表单的 HTML/CSS 中隐藏的一些“谜团”。

与往常一样，尽量不要以“有效”的解决方案来解决。 设计一个解决方案，让每个人的工作都轻松无忧，这也是营销自动化解决方案应该做的。



原文:

Dynamics 365 for Marketing is an advanced marketing automation solution which has seen a great rise in popularity since its unveiling. Because of the complex nature of marketing tools (online marketing, email marketing and real-time marketing) and the fact that D365 Marketing module being "young" in its product lifecycle, this marketing automation solution is deemed to receive more critics than other Dynamics 365 products which are more "mature".

Here is one of the most prominent critiques I have heard working with clients and other D365 Marketing functional consultants:

Customizable can go too far, very involved to develop what is needed. Requires investment in both time and resources. No one seems to know how to get it to work, even the so-called experts.

This pain point is very valid since I have seen a many poorly implemented, customized and managed D365 Marketing instances. In this article, I will use Marketing Forms - one of the most used marketing tools for lead capturing - to prove this point and demonstrate how you can properly implement and maintain D365 Marketing Forms for clients to avoid unnecessary frustration.

The topics discussed in the following section require some knowledge about D365 Marketing Forms

Generally speaking, marketing forms need to follow a client's branding principles so they can be embedded in the client's public facing website seamlessly. In this case, the marketing forms need to styled accordingly unless your client is willing to use a marketing form which looks like it is coming from a 1980's webpage:

If you have used the marketing form designer provided by D365 Marketing, you probably know about - or have already complained about its lack of configurability for styling. Yes you can configure the form's layout, font size, text alignment, text color and paddings for form elements but these functionalities can barely make a web form which is no where near perfect.

Even if you can let the skewed reCAPTCHA block and unstyled button fly, any new fields being added to the marketing form need to be styled individually using the editor user interface. As a functional consultant, you would probably expect that your time is better spent elsewhere.

Luckily, the form designer provides an HTML editor which can be used by web developers (or anyone who has enough knowledge about HTML and CSS) to customize web form appearances to whatever the client requires. However, this customization can also bring us to some very messy scenarios which usually lead to the first part of the quoted critique - "Customizable can go too far so the solution requires investments in both time and resources".

Let's say that you - a functional consultant - has gathered the branding requirements from the customer and added all the fields to the marketing form using the form designer. However, the basic form designer cannot achieve the fancy design of the marketing form so the task is passed to a front-end developer within your development team so the form can be properly styled using custom CSS. This seems like no big deal because every front-end developer (event the juniors) can write CSS and style the form, right?

Unfortunaly, this implementation is very concerning, coming from a solution design perspective.

  • CSS can be added inline of any HTML element and it can also be added between the style tags using class names. If developers on your development team are not following the exact same enforced coding patterns and class naming principles, this implementation can lead to very a bloaded and messy HTML/CSS code base for all the marketing forms available
  • If the marketing form styling needs to be updated following a client's feedback (which happens very frequently) and the task is assigned to another developer, he/she might not be able to follow what the previous dev has done and simply proceeds to add his/her own CSS styling to override the previous styles, which leads to more unnecessary lines of code
  • If a new unstyled field is added to the existig form or if there is another similar form which needs some custom styling, you and your developers need to rinse and repeat this painfully inefficient and messy process.

If we go down this route, we are relying on a group of people's (from all senority levels) diligence just to implement and maintain ONE marketing form. Also, the functional consultants (without solid HTML/CSS experience) will ALWAYS rely on the developers for custom styling changes. Furthermore, If people made the code base a giant pile of spaghetti, it'll be impossible for technical supports to troubleshoot and often customers quote "no one knows what is going on, even the so-called experts",

What could be a more elegant solution? Make use of form templates and custom attributes to enable additional form designer options for non-technical D365 Marketing users.

Sometimes we do criticize Microsoft for poorly documented features but this would not be one of those occasions: the custom attribute feature is documented here under MS Docs for Dynamics 365 Marketing.

To summarize the implementation process, one of your developers would need to add all the commonly used form elements (text fields, dropdown fields, radio input, etc.) to a marketing form template and enable custom configuration attributes such as the gap between each form field and the color of the input labels. This can be done by adding meta tags to the form template's HTML and changing related CSS properties to a templated format. If you are interested about the details of setup, I will release a guide in the future.

So what are the benefits of this implementation?

  • The types of form fields are definite and the client would only need certain level of control over the form fields (field gaps, button color, button color when hovering, etc.), all of which can be implemented by a developer at once on a marketing form template. This means that you have less dependencies involved so the client does not continuous support on small things like changing a button's background color.

  • Admittedly, this implementation does not completely eliminate the risk of poorly coded CSS, but it does alleviate the severity of the issue by having developers implementing the code base only once.

  • The custom configuration can be generic so that you or the client can simply change, for example, the color or font size of all input labels by modifying only one field. The styling change will also be applied to new fields being added to marketing forms.

  • The migration process to another environment is simle and streamlined. Simply copy the HTML of the marketing form template and paste it into a new marketing form template created in another environment and you can start using the template for new forms.

_Let's see it in action. _

This implementation makes it easier for anyone (even without knowledge of HTML/CSS) to control advanced styling attributes, removing the need of constant support from developers. Also, if a client complains to you about certain configuration not working, you know that it won't be some "mysteries" hidden in HTML/CSS of the form.

As always, try not to settle with a solution which "just works". Design a solution that makes everyone's job hassle-free and it is what a marketing automation solution supposed to do.

Items.Title: 去 - 你好世界 | Go - Hello World
Items.Description:
没有人会像“嘿，我今天很无聊，所以让我们创建一种编程语言”（就像我写这篇文章一样）那样创建一种编程语言。 创建编程语言必须有一些理由。

原因是对先进基础设施的需求不断增长。 早期，与今天相比，应用程序的服务器效率较低。 这是因为今天的基础设施是分布式的，处理器是多核和多线程的。 我们需要充分利用这些高级功能来提高我们的应用程序的效率。

分布式系统和多核架构有什么作用？

它们帮助正在运行的应用程序以并行方式运行。 因此，假设一个应用程序中有上传文件、下载文件和删除文件等 3 个操作。 在大多数现代应用程序中，我们可以同时执行这 3 个操作。 这可以通过分布式系统和多核处理器来实现。

分布式基础设施

让我们看看这样的分布式系统是如何工作的。 分布式系统是一个系统，其中该系统的不同逻辑部分位于不同的机器上，但仍充当单个机器（服务器）。 在我们的示例中，一台机器负责上传文件，一台用于下载文件，另一台用于编辑文件。

多核处理器

让我们看看多核处理器是如何工作的。 假设我们一次下载 10 个文件。 在单核处理器的情况下，这些文件被推入队列。 一次下载一个文件，然后一个接一个地下载。 在多核处理器的情况下，每个核占用一个进程，因此如果有10个核，则1个文件下载时间可以下载10个文件。

这就是现代应用程序如何利用当前的基础架构和架构来实现高性能。

并发

但是使用分布式架构可能会面临一个问题。 考虑这种情况。 您将我们应用程序中的文件共享给另一个具有写入权限的用户。 如果该用户在您尝试编辑该文件时尝试删除该文件，则可能会出现问题。 这可能是一个模棱两可的情况。 理想的情况应该是应用程序一次只允许一个用户删除或编辑文件。

这可以通过并发来实现。 并非所有编程语言默认都支持并发。 但是， Go支持开箱即用的并发。

围棋的优点

  • 简单性- 在 go 中编写程序并运行它们很简单。
  • 与大多数其他编程语言相比， Go使用的资源（内存和处理器）更少。

围棋特点

  • 它是一种编译语言，这意味着Go的每个程序都经过编译，编译器会生成一个扩展名为.exe的二进制文件。 从给定程序生成二进制 (.env)文件所花费的时间称为构建时间。
  • 它是一种独立于平台的语言。 在一个操作系统中编译的二进制文件也可以在其他操作系统中运行。 这个二进制文件是程序执行的原因。 运行程序二进制文件所花费的时间称为运行时间。

好的，这就是基本的东西。 让我们深入研究有趣的东西。 为此，我们需要两件事。 我们机器中的代码编辑器和 Go 语言。

安装 VS 代码

安装 VScode 的链接- 转到此链接，您将看到类似这样的内容。

根据您的操作系统选择并安装。 安装应该是直截了当的。

安装围棋

安装 Go 的链接- 转到此链接并选择要安装的操作系统。

这种安装也相当简单。

要检查安装是否成功，请打开终端/命令提示符并键入go并按Enter 。 如果你能看到这样的东西，那就意味着Go安装成功。

创建一个新文件夹或使用现有文件夹来存储我们的 hello world 程序文件。 我将文件夹命名为hello-world

  • 在 VScode 中打开该文件夹。

    • 每当我们在文件夹中启动一个新的 Go 项目时，我们需要做的第一件事就是在该文件夹中初始化一个项目。 执行此操作的命令是go mod init <project name> 。 在终端/命令提示符或 VScode 内置终端中执行此命令。 建议并且一个好的约定是文件夹和项目具有相同的名称。

    • 上面的命令所做的是它创建了一个名为go.mod的文件。 如果您熟悉nodeJS系列，它类似于package.json文件。 如果您不熟悉它，它只是一个包含项目详细信息的文件，其中包含项目名称、依赖项等。

    • 现在创建一个文件并将其命名为您喜欢的任何名称，但扩展名为.go 。 我将其命名为hello.go

    • 当您第一次在 VScode 中创建扩展名为.go的文件时，它会推荐一些扩展安装，如上图和下图所示。 只需安装所有扩展。 无论你在哪里找到install all ，都这样做。 如果没有install将在那里，请执行此操作。

    • 现在我们已经准备好用 Go编写我们的第一个程序了。 所以，我们走吧。

    来到代码

    大多数编程语言都有一个程序执行开始的起点。 同样， go程序也有一个起点，即main函数。

    简单地说，函数是执行特定任务的孤立代码块。 除非我们指定，否则函数不会与程序的其他部分交互。

    一个函数有 3 个部分。 他们是：

    1. 函数头
    2. 函数参数
    3. 函数体

    它以 go 中的关键字func开头。 关键字是为特定目的保留的特定词，我们不能将它们用于任何其他目的。 函数头还包含我们想要唯一标识和调用它的函数的名称。 语法类似于func <function name>

    函数参数包含在一对括号()中。 我们使用参数将数据传递给函数。 记住我们在上面讨论过的函数是与程序的其他部分隔离的。 因此，我们使用这些函数参数作为传递数据以执行所需任务的媒介。 根据任务和该任务的要求，单个函数可以有任意数量的参数。 假设我们有一个函数执行用户在运行时给出的两个数字的加法。 由于它们是在运行时出现的，我们不能在函数内部对它们进行硬编码。 唯一的方法是在运行应用程序时从用户那里获取输入，并将这些输入传递给执行加法的函数。 我们使用它的参数将这些输入传递给该函数。 因此，在这种情况下，我们的加法函数需要两个参数。 参数语法看起来像(<arg 1>, <arg 2>, ...)

    这是我们将实际逻辑写入指定要执行的任务的地方。 在上面的加法示例中，我们希望我们的函数将两个给定的数字相加。 因此，函数的核心逻辑进入主体内部。 函数的主体包裹在花括号{ }中。

    让我们结合所有内容并编写整个函数的语法。

     func < function name > ( < arg1 > , < arg2 > , ... ) { // function body goes here }

    所以，回到节目主题的起点。 无论程序中有多少行代码或多少函数，程序的执行都是从这里开始的。

    因此， hello world程序中的 main 函数将如下所示：

     package main import "fmt" func main () { fmt . Print ( "Hello world" ) }

    嘿，这里有两行我们没有讨论过。

    是的，我知道。

    第 1 行 - 这是每个程序的必填行，描述了当前程序所属的包。 名字可以是任何东西。 我只是给了main 。 你可以给任何你想要的东西。 任何包名在这里都没有任何区别，但是当我们处理大型项目时，包名确实很重要。 要时刻铭记在心。

    第 2 行 - 说导入一个名为fmt的包。 因为我们使用了一个名为Print的函数来打印一些文本到输出终端，而这个 Print 函数属于 fmt 包，就像我们的 main 函数属于 main 包一样。 这就是为什么在使用函数时我们使用fmt. 指定它属于该包。

    因此，只需在包含在" "中的 Print 函数中提供您想要打印的任何内容。

    编写程序后，保存。

    正如我们之前所说，Go 是一种编译语言，这意味着我们必须编译它给出一个可执行文件然后运行它。 我们能做到这一点。

    但我们也可以直接运行它并使用命令go run <file name>查看输出。 在我们的例子中，它是go run main.go 。

    如果您运行与上面相同的代码，您应该在终端中获得这样的输出。

    结论

    就是这个。 更多关于 Go lang 的内容即将推出，请关注我以查看它们。 如果您觉得这很好，请确保您喜欢并分享。

    另外，我从中学到的资源是： Tech world with nana video - 来自 Tech world with Nana 的视频。

    原文:

    Go lang or simply Go is a programming language developed by Google. No one creates a programming language just like "Hey, I am bored today, so let's create a programming language" (like how I am writing this article). There must be some reason to create a programming language.

    And that reason is the growing needs for the advanced infrastructure. Earlier, the servers of applications used to be less efficient when compared to today's. That is because the infrastructure today is distributed and processors are multi-core and multi-threaded. We need to take full advantage of these advanced features to make our applications more efficient.

    What does distributed system and multi-core architecture do?

    They help running applications run in a parallel manner. So, assume that there are 3 operations in an application such as uploading a file, downloading a file and deleting a file. In most modern applications, we can do these 3 operations simultaneously. This is possible by distributed system and multi-core processor.

    Distributed infrastructure

    Let's see how such distributed system works. A distributed system is a system in which the different logical parts of that system is located on different machines but still act as a single machine (server). In our example, one machine takes care of uploading files, one for downloading files and other for editing files.

    Multi-core processors

    Let's see how a multi-core processor works. Assume that we are downloading 10 files at a time. In case of a single core processor, these files are pushed into a queue. One file is downloaded at a time and one after the other. In case of multi-core processor, each core takes one process, so if there are 10 cores, 10 files can be downloaded in the time of one file download.

    This is how the modern applications can leverage the present infrastructure and architecture for high performance.

    Concurrency

    But there is one problem that can be faced using distributed architecture. Consider this situation. You shared a file in our application to another user with write permissions. It can be a problem if that user tries to delete a file when you are trying to edit that file. It can be an ambiguous situation. An ideal situation should be that application should only let one user either to delete or edit the file at a time.

    This can be achieved through concurrency. Not all programming languages support concurrency by default. But, Go supports concurrency out of the box.

    Pros of Go

    • The build time and run time (if not aware of difference between build time and run time, will be explained below) of go programs is rapid almost same as C/C++.
    • Simplicity - It is simple to write programs in go and run them.
    • Go uses fewer resources (memory and processor) than most other programming languages.

    Go characteristics

    • Go is used in backend to write services which interact with cloud infrastructures or databases or any other services.
    • It is a compiled language which means that every program of Go is compiled and the compiler produces a binary file with .exe extension. The time taken to produce a binary (.env) file from a given program is called build time.
    • It is a platform independent language. A binary file compiled in one OS can be run in other OS as well. This binary file is what makes the program execution. The time taken to run a program binary file is called run time.

    Ok, that's it for the basic stuff. Let's dive into the interesting stuff. For that we need two things. A code editor and Go language in our machine.

    Installing VS code

    Link to install VScode - Go to this link and you will see something like this.

    Choose according to your operating system and install. Installation should be straight forward.

    Installing Go

    Link to install Go - Go to this link and select your operating system to install.

    This installation is also fairly straight forward.

    To check successful installation, open terminal / command prompt and type go and press Enter. If you can see something like this, it means Go installed successfully.

    Hello world in GO

    • Create a new folder or use an existing folder to store our hello world program file. I am naming the folder as hello-world

    • Open that folder in VScode.

    • Whenever we are starting a new Go project in a folder, the first thing we need to do is to initialise a project in that folder. The command to do that is go mod init <project name>. Execute this command in terminal / command prompt or VScode in-built terminal. It is recommended and a good convention is to have same name for both folder and project.

    • What the above command does is that it creates a file called go.mod. If you are familiar with nodeJS family, it is something similar to package.json file. If you are not familiar with it, it's just a file which contains details about the project which contains project name, dependencies, etc.

    • Now create a file and name it whatever you like but with the extension .go. I am naming it as hello.go

    • The moment you create a file with .go extension for the first time in your VScode, it will recommend some extensions to install like in the above picture and also like in the below picture. Just install all the extensions. Wherever you find install all, do that. If not install will be there, do that.

    • Now we are perfectly ready to write our first program in Go. So, let's go.

    Coming to the code

    Most programming languages have a starting point from where the program execution starts. Similarly, go programs have a starting point which is main function.

    To simply put, a function is an isolated block of code which performs a certain task. A function doesn't interact with other parts of program unless we specify it to.

    There are 3 parts of a function. They are:

    1. Function header
    2. Function arguments
    3. Function body

    1. Function header

    It starts with a keyword func in go. And a keyword is a specific word which is reserved for a specific purpose and we can't use them for any other purposes. Function header also contains the name of the function with which we want to uniquely identify and call it. The syntax will be like func <function name>

    2. Function arguments

    Function arguments are wrapped inside a pair of parenthesis (). We use arguments to pass data to a function. Remember from the upper part where we discussed that a function is isolated from other parts of program. So, we use these function arguments as a medium to pass data in order to perform required task. There can be any number of arguments for a single function depending upon the task and requirements of that task. Let's say we have a function which performs addition of two numbers which a user gives in run time. Since they come in run time, we can't hardcode them inside function. The only way is to take input from user while running application and pass those inputs to a function which performs addition. And we pass those inputs to that function using its arguments. So, in this case, our addition function requires two arguments. The arguments syntax look like (<arg 1>, <arg 2>, ...)

    3. Function body

    This is the place where we write actual logic to the task it is specified to do. In above addition example, we want our function to add two given numbers. Hence, the core logic of the function goes inside the body. The body of a function is wrapped inside curly braces { }.

    Let's combine everything and write the syntax of entire function.

    func <function name> (<arg1>, <arg2>, ...) { // function body goes here
}

    So, coming back to the starting point of program topic. Starting point of a go program is a special function named main. And the program execution starts here no matter how many lines of code or how many functions are there in the program.

    So, the main function in hello world program is going to look like:

    package main import "fmt" func main() { fmt.Print("Hello world")
}

    Hey, here are two lines those we haven't discussed about.

    Yeah... I know.

    The line 1 - This is a mandatory line for every program which depicts the package to which the current program belongs to. The name can be anything. I just gave main. You can give whatever you want. Any package name doesn't make any difference here but when we work on huge projects, the package names do matter. Just keep that in mind.

    The line 2 - Says import a package called fmt. Because we are using a function called Print to print some text to output terminal and this Print function belongs to fmt package like how our main function belongs to main package. That's why while using the function we are using fmt. to specify that it belongs to that package.

    So, just give whatever you want to print inside that Print function wrapped in " ".

    After writing program, save it.

    As we talked before, Go is a compiled language, which means that we have to compile which gives an executable file and then run it. We can do that.

    But we can also just directly run it and see the output using the command go run <file name>. In our case, it is go run main.go.

    If you ran same code as above, you should be getting output like this in your terminal.

    Conclusion

    That's it for this one. More on Go lang will be coming soon and do follow me to see them. Make sure you like and share if you felt this good.

    Also, the resource I learnt from is: Tech world with nana video - a video from Tech world with Nana.

Items.Description:
在游戏开发方面，我自己也是初学者。 这可能不是最好的方法，但我没有在网上找到另一种方法，所以这可能对新手有帮助。

如果您发现任何错误或有更好的解决方案，请在评论中指出。

目标

所以，我想要创建的是一个着色器，我的意思是，一种具有重复纹理的材质，我可以分配给一个立方体，然后根据需要调整它的大小。 它必须在每一面看起来都一样，并且瓷砖的大小必须是恒定的。

只是从一开始就清楚，据我所知，这只有在您使用立方体时才能正常工作。

创建纹理

对于此示例，我决定使用Rectangle和Eclipse节点创建一个带有黑色边框和中心小圆圈的简单纹理。

我将假设您已经知道如何创建着色器图形着色器，将其分配给材质，并将该材质分配给对象。

我还将假设这部分不需要任何解释。

这是调整大小的立方体上的纹理。

平铺效果

创建平铺效果非常简单； 我们只需要一个Tiling And Offset节点，连接到Fraction节点，然后我们连接到我们的纹理节点，在本例中为Rectangle和Eclipse 。

然后我们可以用平铺 Vector2 来选择平铺的数量。 还有一个偏移量 Vector2，但我不会在这里使用那个。

我们可以看到效果有效，但这仍然不是我们想要的。

使瓦片大小保持不变

这当然是最困难的部分。 然而，由于我们使用调整大小的立方体作为我们的对象，我们可以利用对象的比例来确定瓦片的数量。

在着色器图中获取对象的比例非常容易。 它是我们从Object节点获得的两个属性之一。

因此，要获得面上的瓷砖数量，我们可以将比例除以瓷砖大小。

为了定义图块大小，我创建了一个 Vector2 属性。 在此示例中，我将其设置为 0.8。

你可能已经注意到有一个小问题，scale 是 Vector3 属性，而 tiling 是 Vector2 属性，那么我们将如何选择我们需要的两个轴呢？

为了解决这个问题，我们需要法线向量，我们可以通过法线向量节点访问它。 法线向量垂直于纹理面。 这意味着如果我们反转然后将法线向量与比例相乘，我们将得到一个向量3，其中不相关的轴为零。

确保在此过程中的某处使用绝对节点以避免负值。 此外，将法线向量空间设置为Object 。 否则，旋转立方体会破坏你的瓷砖。

最后要做的就是从我们的 Vector3 中删除 0，从而将它变成一个可以连接到平铺的 Vector2。

我不知道如何解决这个问题，所以我只是重用了我为其他东西创建的自定义节点，它需要三个输入并返回两个最大的输入。

如果您想使用与我相同的方法，请创建一个自定义节点，为其提供三个浮点输入（A、B、C）和两个浮点输出（X、Y）。 将类型设置为String并将此代码放入Body中。

 bool a = A < B ; if ( a ) { bool c = C < A ; Y = B ; X = c ? A : C ; } else { bool b = C > B ; X = A ; Y = b ? C : B ; }

就是这样！ 我们的立方体现在覆盖着同样大的瓷砖。 我们可以使用这个着色器来创建墙壁、地板等等。

这是我对最后一部分的实现。

这是我们的立方体。 如果您希望瓷砖完美贴合表面，那么，不要切割瓷砖（如图所示），确保立方体尺寸可被瓷砖尺寸整除。

感谢您的阅读！



原文:

I am a beginner myself when it comes to game dev. This may not be the best way to do this, but I failed to find another method online, so this might help a fellow newbie.

If you notice any mistakes or have better solutions, please point them out in the comments.

The goal

So, what I wanted to create is a shader, I mean, a material with a repeating texture I could just assign to a cube and then resize it however I want. It had to look the same on every side, and the tile size had to be constant.

Just to be clear from the beginning, as far as I know, this will work correctly only if you are using a cube.

Creating a texture

For this example, I decided to create a simple texture with a black border and a small circle in the center using the Rectangle and Eclipse node.

I am going to assume you already know how to create a shader graph shader, assign it to a material, and assign that material to an object.

I am also going to assume that this part doesn't require any explaining.

Here is the texture on a resized cube.

Tiling effect

Creating a tiling effect is pretty straightforward; all we need is a Tiling And Offset node, connected to the Fraction node, which we then connect to our texture nodes, in this case, Rectangle and Eclipse.

We can then mess with tiling Vector2 to choose the number of tiles. There is also an offset Vector2, but I won't be using that one here.

We can see that the effect works, but this is still not what we want.

Making tile size constant

This is, of course, the most difficult part. However, since we are using a resized cube as our object, we can make use of the object's scale to determine the number of tiles.

Getting an object's scale in shader graph is pretty easy. It's one of two properties we get from the Object node.

So, to get the number of tiles on a face, we can divide the scale by the tile size.

To define the tile size I created a Vector2 property. In this example, I set it to .8.

You might have noticed that there is one small problem, scale is a Vector3 property, and tiling is a Vector2 property, so how will we pick which two axis we need?

To solve this issue we need the normal vector, which we can access with the Normal Vector node. The normal vector is perpendicular to the texture face. That means that if we invert and then multiply the normal vector with the scale, we will get a Vector3 with the irrelevant axis as zero.

Make sure you use the Absolute node somewhere in this process to avoid negative values. Also, set the normal vector space to Object. Otherwise, rotating the cube will ruin your tiles.

The last thing to do is just removing the 0 from our Vector3, thus turning it into a Vector2 we can connect to tiling.

I wasn't sure how to approach this problem so I just reused a custom node I created for something else which takes three inputs and returns the two largest ones.

If you want to use the same method I did, create a custom node, give it three float inputs (A, B, C) and two float outputs (X, Y). Set the type to String and put this code in Body.

bool a = A < B; if (a) {
bool c=C<A;
Y=B;
X=c?A:C;
} else {
bool b=C>B;
X=A;
Y=b?C:B;
}

And that's it! Our cube is now covered with equally large tiles. And we can use this shader to create walls, floors, and whatnot.

Here is my implementation of that last part.

And this is our cube. :D

If you want the tiles to perfectly fit the surface, so, no cut tiles (like in the picture), make sure the cube size is divisible by the tile size.

Thank you for reading!

    从代码执行结果可以看出，没有区别。 所以只要选择你写作的语言。 如何选择框架？ 当然，自己编写 API 请求也不错。 它减少了对第三方库的依赖，并允许您更多地控制代码的行为。 但是当这样的方法超过二十个时，它已经增加了代码的大小。 管理所有逻辑变得困难。 这就是第三方库（框架）来救援的地方。 选择语言后，您可以在官方 Telegram 页面上的列表中考虑不同库的选项。 对于JavaScript ，我建议使用node-telegram-bot-api和telegraf 。 对于Python ，您可以使用pyTelegramBotAPI和aiogram （强烈推荐）。

    让我们开始编写第一个机器人🐱‍💻

    由于我主要使用 Python 编写代码，我建议您开始使用aiogram开发机器人。 它可以帮助您使您的机器人更快、更简单。

    1. 安装软件包。 我们可以通过pip做到这一点。 此外，此处列出了下载库的其他方法。

       pip install -U aiogram

    2. 让我们创建一个项目结构。 我们需要两个 python 文件： config.py和main.py 。 它看起来像这样：

    3. 获取令牌。 令牌是一个110201543:AAHdqTcvCH1vGWJxfSeofSAs0K5PALDsaw的字符串，它是授权 bot 并向Bot API发送请求所必需的。 确保您的令牌安全并安全存储，任何人都可以使用它来控制您的机器人。 要创建一个新的，您需要与@BotFather “交谈”并在那里获得一个令牌。

    4. 写一些启动代码。 首先，从BotFater复制令牌并将其粘贴到config.py中。 我们使用os模块来处理环境变量。 将令牌保密是一个很好的解决方案。 当您处于开发阶段时，您可以看到令牌。 但在生产阶段，将其删除。

       import os API_TOKEN = os . getenv ( 'TOKEN' , '5135575762:AAF9vytvlcuL-hruAHHMgZ3G2WvLpbZXMSI' )

      在main.py中，我们需要从config.py导入logging模块、 aiogram objects和API_TOKEN 。

       import logging from aiogram import Bot , Dispatcher , executor from aiogram.types import Message from config import API_TOKEN

      现在让我们初始化bot和dispatcher对象：

       # Configure logging logging . basicConfig ( level = logging . INFO ) # Initialize bot and dispatcher bot = Bot ( token = API_TOKEN ) dp = Dispatcher ( bot )

      下一步：与机器人的交互从一个命令开始。 我们可以像这样处理来自用户的命令：

       @ dp . message_handler ( commands = [ 'start' , 'help' ]) async def handle_start ( message : Message ) -> Message : return await message . reply ( "Hi! \n I'm TestBot!" )

      此外，我们还添加了一个处理程序来捕获所有文本消息：

       @ dp . message_handler () async def echo ( message : Message ) -> Message : return await message . answer ( message . text )

      最后一步：运行长轮询。 此命令将运行我们的机器人以每秒捕获来自 Telegram API 的所有更新：

       if __name__ == '__main__' : executor . start_polling ( dp , skip_updates = True )

    5. 总之，我们有两个文件：

     import os API_TOKEN = os . getenv ( 'TOKEN' , '5135575762:AAF9vytvlcuL-hruAHHMgZ3G2WvLpbZXMSI' )
     import logging from aiogram import Bot , Dispatcher , executor from aiogram.types import Message from config import API_TOKEN # Configure logging logging . basicConfig ( level = logging . INFO ) # Initialize bot and dispatcher bot = Bot ( token = API_TOKEN ) dp = Dispatcher ( bot ) @ dp . message_handler ( commands = [ 'start' , 'help' ]) async def handle_start ( message : Message ) -> Message : return await message . reply ( "Hi! \n I'm TestBot!" ) @ dp . message_handler () async def echo ( message : Message ) -> Message : return await message . answer ( message . text ) if __name__ == '__main__' : executor . start_polling ( dp , skip_updates = True )

    我们最后得到了什么？🤩

    让我们在终端中使用命令运行我们的机器人：

    在控制台中，你会是这样的：

     INFO:aiogram:Bot: testbot [@testmezgoodlebot] WARNING:aiogram:Updates were skipped successfully. INFO:aiogram.dispatcher.dispatcher:Start polling.

    现在让我们去电报并开始与机器人聊天

    要开始与机器人聊天，请点击 BotFather 提供的链接。

    我希望，你有像我一样的结果😺

    恭喜！ 你已经编写了你的​​第一个机器人！ 是的，现在这是一个简单的 echo-bot，但在以后的文章中，我们将涵盖机器人开发的所有方面。 然后，您已经可以创建任何复杂性的机器人。 所以，等待我的下一个新帖子。

    原文:

    Maksym Zavalniuk

    Posted on Aug 14

    Telegram has a huge API interface, and the development of bots is currently increasing very quickly and is in great demand. For beginners, this is a nice opportunity to get the first job and a good salary. The question arises: how to start writing a bot?

    ✔In this post, you'll find information on:

    • which language to choose;
    • which frameworks to use;
    • how to improve bot development;
    • what to do next.

    How to choose the language? 🤔

    Honestly, write in the language that is closest to you. There is no gold standard. Let's see why. As you know, to get data from an API, you need to write a script that makes HTTP requests. For example, getting data from GitHub API, it will look like this:

    • in Python (with requests package) 
    >>> response = requests.get('https://api.github.com')
>>> response.content

    As you can see from the code execution results, there is no difference. So just choose the language in which you write. 😊

    How to choose the framework? 🏎️

    Of course, it's good to write API requests yourself. It reduces the dependency on third-party libraries and allows you to control the behavior of the code more. But when there are more such methods than twenty, it already increases the size of the code. It becomes difficult to manage all the logic. This is where third-party libraries(frameworks) come to the rescue. After choosing a language, you can consider the options of different libraries from the list here, on the official Telegram page. For JavaScript I recommend using node-telegram-bot-api and telegraf. For Python you can take pyTelegramBotAPI and aiogram(highly recommended).

    Let's start coding the first bot 🐱‍💻

    Since I mostly write code in Python, I suggest you start developing bots with the aiogram. Aiogram is a pretty simple and fully asynchronous framework for Telegram Bot API written in Python 3.7 with asyncio and aiohttp. It helps you to make your bots faster and simpler.

    1. Install the package. We can do this by pip. Also, other ways to download the library are listed here.

      pip install -U aiogram

    2. Let's create a project structure. We need two python files: config.py and main.py. It will look like this:

    3. Getting the token. The token is a string along the lines of 110201543:AAHdqTcvCH1vGWJxfSeofSAs0K5PALDsaw that is required to authorize the bot and send requests to the Bot API. Keep your token secure and store it safely, it can be used by anyone to control your bot. To create a new one, you need to "talk" to the @BotFather and get a token there.

    4. Write some start code. First of all, copy the token from the BotFater and paste it to the config.py. We use the os module to work with environment variables. It's a good solution to keep your token secret. While you are in the development stage, the token can be visible to you. But in the production stage, remove it.

      import os API_TOKEN = os.getenv('TOKEN', '5135575762:AAF9vytvlcuL-hruAHHMgZ3G2WvLpbZXMSI')

      In the main.py we need to import the logging module, aiogram objects, and API_TOKEN from config.py.

      import logging from aiogram import Bot, Dispatcher, executor
from aiogram.types import Message from config import API_TOKEN

      Now let's initialize bot and dispatcher objects:

      # Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO) # Initialize bot and dispatcher
bot = Bot(token=API_TOKEN)
dp = Dispatcher(bot)

      Next step: interaction with bots starts with one command. We can handle commands from users like this:

      @dp.message_handler(commands=['start', 'help'])
async def handle_start(message: Message) -> Message: return await message.reply("Hi!\nI'm TestBot!")

      Also, we add one more handler to catch all text messages:

      @dp.message_handler()
async def echo(message: Message) -> Message: return await message.answer(message.text)

      The last step: run long polling. This command will run our bot to catch all updates from Telegram API every second:

      if __name__ == '__main__': executor.start_polling(dp, skip_updates=True)

    5. In summary, we have two files:

    import os API_TOKEN = os.getenv('TOKEN', '5135575762:AAF9vytvlcuL-hruAHHMgZ3G2WvLpbZXMSI')

    import logging from aiogram import Bot, Dispatcher, executor
from aiogram.types import Message from config import API_TOKEN # Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO) # Initialize bot and dispatcher
bot = Bot(token=API_TOKEN)
dp = Dispatcher(bot) @dp.message_handler(commands=['start', 'help'])
async def handle_start(message: Message) -> Message: return await message.reply("Hi!\nI'm TestBot!") @dp.message_handler()
async def echo(message: Message) -> Message: return await message.answer(message.text) if __name__ == '__main__': executor.start_polling(dp, skip_updates=True)

    What we got in the end?🤩

    Let's run our bot with the command in the terminal:

    In the console you will something like this:

    INFO:aiogram:Bot: testbot [@testmezgoodlebot]
WARNING:aiogram:Updates were skipped successfully.
INFO:aiogram.dispatcher.dispatcher:Start polling.

    Now let's go to the Telegram and start chatting with the bot

    To start a chat with the bot, follow the link provided by BotFather.

    I hope, you have a result like mine 😺

    Congratulations! You've written your first bot! ✨

    Yes, now this is a simple echo-bot, but in future posts, we will cover all aspects of bot development. Then you can already create bots of any complexity. So, wait for my next new posts.

    References:

    Thank you for reading! ❤️ ❤️ ❤️

Items.Title: 使用 Sinatra 和 ActiveRecord 的 Ruby 应用程序的典型文件夹结构 | Typical folder structure for a Ruby application using Sinatra and ActiveRecord
Items.Description:
海梅·蒙特斯·德奥卡

发表于在编写软件应用程序时，关注点分离非常重要，尤其是在面向对象编程中。 关键是我们使用易于使用的文件夹结构，并清楚地让我们知道将每个文件放在哪里。

这是关于在使用Sinatra和Active Record在Ruby中编码时如何制作此文件夹结构的建议。 此结构中的每个文件夹以及其中的每个文件都有明确定义的职责。

根文件夹中的文件：
文件夹：
在这里，我们定义了如何连接到数据库并需要我们的应用程序所需的文件或 gem。
这是用于生成示例数据的有用 gem 。 我们可以使用 rake 任务db:seed将该样本数据添加到数据库中。
遵循精心设计的文件夹结构，可以根据职责轻松了解将代码放置在何处，有助于我们的应用程序在可控范围内增长，并帮助其他开发人员在未来轻松维护应用程序。

原文:

Jaime Montes de Oca

Posted on Aug 14

When writing a software application, separation of concerns is very important, especially with object oriented programming. It is key that we use a folder structure that is easy to work with and clearly let us know where to put each file.

This is a suggestion on how to make this folder structure when you are coding in Ruby using Sinatra and Active Record. Each folder in this structure has a clearly defined responsibility as well as each file inside of them.

Files in the root folder:
Gemfile: Lists all the gems that need to be installed for our application to run.
Rakefile: Contains code we create and use for common tasks we can run from the command line and also by requiring sinatra/activerecord/rake we can have access to other useful tasks like db:migrate or db:seed.

Folders:
app/models: Here we put all our Active Record models, the core of our application because here we access and update the database responding to the user actions.
app/controllers: Following the software pattern known as Model-View-Controller, this folder contains the code for creating our API end points that are responsible for feeding the client app.
config: Code responsible for our environment setup. Here we define how to connect to the database and require files or gems needed by our application.
db: Has the different databases we are using for each environment (development/test).
db/migrate: Files to create/alter the database structure.
db/seed.rb: File that contains code to add sample data to our database. Here is a useful gem for generating sample data. We can use the rake task db:seed to add that sample data to the database.
spec: Contains all the test files.

Following a well designed folder structure will make it easy to know where to put code depending of its responsibility, will help our application to grow under control and will help other developers to easily maintain the application in the future.

Items.Title: 根据用户权限在 Angular 应用中采用 UX/UI 的灵活方式。 | The flexible way to adopt UX/UI in Angular app according to user’s permissions.
Items.Description:
在许多项目中，都有身份验证过程（或多或少）。 许多“最佳实践”是用所有已知技术编写的，依此类推。

但是用户登录后会发生什么？ 毕竟，他目前还不能做所有事情。 如何定义他能看到的和不能看到的。 他有权点击哪些按钮，更改、创建和删除什么。

在本文中，我想考虑在 Web 应用程序中用于解决这些问题的方法。

首先，当前/有效授权只能在服务器上进行。 在前端，我们只能改进 UI 和 UX。 我们可以隐藏用户无权点击的按钮，或者阻止他访问页面，或者显示他无权执行特定操作的消息。

这里出现了一个问题，如何使其尽可能正确？ 让我们从定义问题开始。

我们创建了一个Todo App ，它有不同类型的用户：

  • 在这种情况下，我们可以使用“角色”。 但是，情况可能会发生很大变化。 想象一下拥有删除任务权限的 ADMIN 用户。 或 USER 有权查看统计信息。 一个简单的解决方案是创建新角色。

    但是在大型应用程序中，拥有庞大的用户角色系统，我们很快就会迷失在大量的角色中……

    在这里我们回顾一下“用户权限”权限。 为了便于管理，我们可以创建多个权限的组并将它们附加给用户。 始终可以为特定用户添加特定权限。

    这种解决方案可以在许多大型服务中找到：AWS、Google Cloud、SalesForce 等。 此外，类似的解决方案已经在许多框架中实现，例如 Django (Python)。

    我想举一个 Angular 应用程序的实现示例。 首先，让我们定义所有可能的权限。

    1. 划分为特征。 我们有任务和统计数据。
    2. 我们用它们中的每一个定义可能的动作。 任务：创建、读取、更新、删除。 统计数据：读取（在我们的示例中仅查看）。
    3. 创建角色和权限的 MAP。
     export const permissionsMap = { todos :{ create : ' * ' , read : ' * ' , update : ' * ' , delete : ' * ' }, stats : ' * ' }

    在我看来，这是最好的选择，但在大多数情况下，服务器会返回如下内容：

     export permissions = [ ' todos_create ' , ' todos_read ' , ' todos_update ' , ' todos_delete ' , ' stats ' , ]

    可读性较差，但也不差。

    这是当用户已经通过身份验证但根据用户的权限尚未采用 UI/UX 时我们的应用程序的外观：

    让我们看看 USER 的权限：

     export const USERpermissionsMap = { todos :{ read : ' * ' , update : ' * ' , } }

    导航：

    对于 Angular 中的这种情况，有 Guards 用于路由级别（文档）。

     @ Injectable ({ providedIn : ' root ' }) export class PermissionsGuardService implements CanActivate { constructor () { } canActivate ( route : ActivatedRouteSnapshot , state : RouterStateSnapshot ): Observable < boolean > | boolean { const userPerms = getPermissions (); const required = route . data . permission ; const isPermitted = checkPermissions ( required , userPerms ); if ( ! isPermitted ) { alert ( ' ROUTE GUARD SAYS: \n You don \' t have permissions to see this page ' ); } return isPermitted ; } }

    注意data.permissions = 'stats'中的对象，这些正是用户需要拥有的权限才能访问此页面。

    一个帮助函数，它返回一个具有用户权限的对象。
    checkPermissions(); 检查所需权限是否是用户权限的一部分。

     export function getPermissions () { let userPerms ; // In our example we using stor as a single source of truth. // Full implementation can be found here https://github.com/danduh/ngx-to-do-permissions. const store : Store < any > = AppInjector . get ( Store ); store . pipe ( select ( userPermissionsState ), take ( 1 ) ) . subscribe (( _p ) => { userPerms = _p ? _p : {}; }); return userPerms ; } export function checkPermissions ( required : string , userPerms ) { // 1) Separate feature and action const [ feature , action ] = required . split ( ' _ ' ); // 2) Check if user have any type of access to the feature if ( ! userPerms . hasOwnProperty ( feature )) { return false ; } // 3) Check if user have permission for required action if ( ! userPerms [ feature ]. hasOwnProperty ( action )) { return false ; } return true ; }

    隐藏元素

    现在，我们的 USER 无法导航到统计页面。 但是，他仍然可以创建任务并删除它们。

    要使 USER 无法删除任务，只需从任务行中删除红色 (X) 即可。

    为此，我们将创建一个自定义结构指令。

     @ Directive ({ selector : ' [appPermissions] ' }) export class PermissionsDirective { private _required : string ; private _viewRef : EmbeddedViewRef < any > | null = null ; private _templateRef : TemplateRef < any > | null = null ; @ Input () set appPermissions ( permission : string ) { this . _required = permission ; this . _viewRef = null ; this . init (); } constructor ( private templateRef : TemplateRef < any > , private viewContainerRef : ViewContainerRef ) { this . _templateRef = templateRef ; } init () { const userPerms = getPermmisions (); const isPermitted = checkPermissions ( this . _required , userPerms ); if ( isPermitted ) { this . _viewRef = this . viewContainerRef . createEmbeddedView ( this . templateRef ); } else { console . log ( ' PERMISSIONS DIRECTIVE says \n You don \' t have permissions to see it ' ); } } }
     <!-- Similar to other Structural Directives in angular (*ngIF, *ngFor..) we have '*' in directive name 'todos_delete' -> is an Input() for directive as a required permission to see this btn. --> <button *appPermissions= "'todos_delete'" class= "delete-button" (click)= "removeSingleTodo()" > X </button>

    现在 USER 看不到 DELETE 按钮，但仍然可以添加新任务。

    采用元素状态：

    如果我们删除输入字段，我们的应用程序的整体外观将被破坏。 在这种情况下，正确的解决方案是禁用输入字段。

    让我们试试这个管道。

     <!-- as a value `permissions` pipe will get required permission `permissions` pipe return true or false, that's why we have !('todos_create' | permissions) to set disable=true if pipe returns false --> <input class= "centered-block" [disabled]= "!('todos_create' | permissions)" placeholder= "What needs to be done?" autofocus />
     @ Pipe ({ name : ' permissions ' }) export class PermissionsPipe implements PipeTransform { constructor () { } transform ( required : any , args ?: any ): any { const userPerms = getPermissions (); const isPermitted = checkPermissions ( required , userPerms ); if ( isPermitted ) { return true ; } else { console . log ( ' [PERMISSIONS PIPE] You don \' t have permissions ' ); return false ; } } }

    现在用户只能看到任务并更改它们（选中/取消选中）。 但是，我们仍然有完全功能的清除完成按钮可供用户使用。

    装饰器：

    假设我们的产品经理有以下要求：

    1. 它也应该是可点击的。

    2. 如果没有相应权限的用户按下按钮，则会出现错误消息。

    施工指令不会帮助我们，管道也不会。

    将权限检查注册到组件函数中也不是很方便。 我们只需要检查点击和绑定函数执行之间的权限。

    在我看来，这里值得使用装饰器。

     @ Component ({ selector : ' app-actions ' , templateUrl : ' ./actions.component.html ' , styleUrls : [ ' ./actions.component.css ' ] }) export class ActionsComponent implements OnInit { @ Output () deleteCompleted = new EventEmitter (); constructor () { } // Our custom decorator, as a param we pass required permission @ Permissions ( ' todos_delete ' ) public deleteCompleted () { this . deleteCompleted . emit (); } }
     export function Permissions ( required ) { return ( classProto , propertyKey , descriptor ) => { const originalFunction = descriptor . value ; descriptor . value = function (... args : any []) { const userPerms = getPermissions (); const isPermitted = checkPermissions ( required , userPerms ); if ( isPermitted ) { originalFunction . apply ( this , args ); } else { // Should throw/log error, but for better visibility will use alert() alert ( ' PERMISSIONS DECORATOR says \n you have no permissions ' ); } }; return descriptor ; }; }

    我们的最终结果：

    结论

    也许这不是最好的解决方案。 但是这种方法允许我们根据用户拥有的权限轻松、动态地调整我们的 UX。

原文:

In many projects, there are authentication processes (more or less). A lot of “best practices” were written in all known technologies and so on, and so forth.

But what happens after the user made the login? After all, he by far cannot do everything. How to define what he can see and what not. What buttons he has the rights to click on, what to change, create and delete.

In this article, I want to consider the approach used for solving these problems in a web application.

To begin with, the present/effective authorization can take place only on the server. On front-end, we are able just to improve UI and UX. We can hide the buttons on which the user has no rights to click, or prevent him from reaching the pages, or to show the message that he does not have the rights to do a certain action.

And here arises the question, how to make it as correctly as possible? Let’s start by defining the problem.

We created a Todo App and it has different types of users:

  • USER — can see and update all the tasks (check/uncheck), but cannot delete, create and see statistics page.

  • ADMIN — can see all the tasks and create new ones, but cannot see statistics.

  • SUPER_ADMIN — can see all the tasks, create new ones and delete them, and also can see statistics.

In this a situation, we can use the “roles”. However, the situation can change a lot. Imagine ADMIN user with permissions to delete tasks. Or USER with access to see statistics. A simple solution is to create new roles.

But on large applications, with massive user role system, we will quickly get lost in a huge amount of roles…

And here we recall the “user rights” permissions. For easier management, we can create groups of several permissions and attach them to the user. It is always possible to add specific permission to a specific user.

This kind of solutions can be found in many large services: AWS, Google Cloud, SalesForce and so on. Also, similar solutions have already been implemented in many frameworks, for example, Django (Python).

I want to give an example of implementation for Angular applications. (On the example of the same ToDo App).

First, let’s define all possible permissions.

  1. Divide into features. We have tasks and statistics.
  2. We define possible actions with each of them. Task: create, read, update, delete. Statistics: read (in our example only viewing).
  3. Create a MAP of roles and permissions. 
export const permissionsMap = { todos:{ create:'*', read:'*', update:'*', delete:'*' }, stats:'*'
}

In my opinion, this is the best option, but in most cases, the server will return something like this:

export permissions = [ 'todos_create', 'todos_read', 'todos_update', 'todos_delete', 'stats',
]

Less readable, but also not bad at all.

This is how our application looks when the user is already authenticated but UI/UX is not yet adopted according to user’s permissions:

Let’s look at the USER’s permissions:

export const USERpermissionsMap = { todos:{ read:'*', update:'*', }
}

Navigation:

USER cannot see the statistics, it means he basically cannot navigate to this page.

For such situations in Angular, there are Guards, which are used at the Routes level (documentation).

@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root'
})
export class PermissionsGuardService implements CanActivate { constructor() { } canActivate(route: ActivatedRouteSnapshot, state: RouterStateSnapshot): Observable<boolean> | boolean { const userPerms = getPermissions(); const required = route.data.permission; const isPermitted = checkPermissions(required, userPerms); if (!isPermitted) { alert('ROUTE GUARD SAYS: \n You don\'t have permissions to see this page'); } return isPermitted; }
}

Pay attention to the object in data.permissions = 'stats', these are exactly the permission that the user needs to have, in order to have access to this page.

Based on the required data.permissions and USER’s permissions PermissionsGuardService will decide whether or not to allow access on page ‘/stats’.

getPermissions(); a helper function that returns an object with user's permissions.
checkPermissions(); checks if required permission is part of user’s permissions.

export function getPermissions() { let userPerms; // In our example we using stor as a single source of truth.  // Full implementation can be found here https://github.com/danduh/ngx-to-do-permissions. const store: Store<any> = AppInjector.get(Store); store .pipe( select(userPermissionsState), take(1) ) .subscribe((_p) => { userPerms = _p ? _p : {}; }); return userPerms;
} export function checkPermissions(required: string, userPerms) { // 1) Separate feature and action const [feature, action] = required.split('_'); // 2) Check if user have any type of access to the feature if (!userPerms.hasOwnProperty(feature)) { return false; } // 3) Check if user have permission for required action if (!userPerms[feature].hasOwnProperty(action)) { return false; } return true;
}

Hiding Elements

Now, our USER cannot navigate to the statistics page. However, he still can create tasks and delete them.

To make USER unable to delete tasks, it will be enough to remove the red (X) from the task line.

For this purpose, we will create a custom Structural Directive.

@Directive({ selector: '[appPermissions]'
})
export class PermissionsDirective { private _required: string; private _viewRef: EmbeddedViewRef<any> | null = null; private _templateRef: TemplateRef<any> | null = null; @Input() set appPermissions(permission: string) { this._required = permission; this._viewRef = null; this.init(); } constructor(private templateRef: TemplateRef<any>, private viewContainerRef: ViewContainerRef) { this._templateRef = templateRef; } init() { const userPerms = getPermmisions(); const isPermitted = checkPermissions(this._required, userPerms); if (isPermitted) { this._viewRef = this.viewContainerRef.createEmbeddedView(this.templateRef); } else { console.log('PERMISSIONS DIRECTIVE says \n You don\'t have permissions to see it'); } }
}

<!-- Similar to other Structural Directives in angular (*ngIF, *ngFor..) we have '*' in directive name 'todos_delete' -> is an Input() for directive as a required permission to see this btn.
-->
<button *appPermissions="'todos_delete'" class="delete-button" (click)="removeSingleTodo()"> X
</button>

Now USER does not see the DELETE button but can still add new tasks.

Adopt element state:

If we remove the input field, the whole look of our application will ruin. The correct solution in this situation would be to disable the input field.

Let’s try pipe for this.

<!-- as a value `permissions` pipe will get required permission `permissions` pipe return true or false, that's why we have !('todos_create' | permissions) to set disable=true if pipe returns false -->
<input class="centered-block" [disabled]="!('todos_create' | permissions)" placeholder="What needs to be done?" autofocus/>

@Pipe({ name: 'permissions'
})
export class PermissionsPipe implements PipeTransform { constructor() { } transform(required: any, args?: any): any { const userPerms = getPermissions(); const isPermitted = checkPermissions(required, userPerms); if (isPermitted) { return true; } else { console.log('[PERMISSIONS PIPE] You don\'t have permissions'); return false; } }
}

And now the USER can only see the tasks and change them (check/uncheck). However, we still have Clear completed button fully functional and available for USER.

Decorators:

Suppose that we have the following requirements from our Product Manager:

  1. The ‘Clear Completed’ button should be visible to everyone and always.

  2. It should also be clickable.

  3. In case USER without corresponding permissions presses the button, an error message should appear.

Constructional directive will not help us, nor does pipes.

It is also not very convenient to register the check for permissions into component functions. All we need is to check permissions between the click and the execution of the bound function.

In my opinion here it is worth to use the decorators.

@Component({ selector: 'app-actions', templateUrl: './actions.component.html', styleUrls: ['./actions.component.css']
})
export class ActionsComponent implements OnInit { @Output() deleteCompleted = new EventEmitter(); constructor() { } // Our custom decorator, as a param we pass required permission @Permissions('todos_delete') public deleteCompleted() { this.deleteCompleted.emit(); }
}

export function Permissions(required) { return (classProto, propertyKey, descriptor) => { const originalFunction = descriptor.value; descriptor.value = function (...args: any[]) { const userPerms = getPermissions(); const isPermitted = checkPermissions(required, userPerms); if (isPermitted) { originalFunction.apply(this, args); } else { // Should throw/log error, but for better visibility will use alert() alert('PERMISSIONS DECORATOR says \n you have no permissions'); } }; return descriptor; };
}

And our final result:

Conclusion

Maybe it’s not the best solutions. But this approach allows us to easily and dynamically adapt our UX in accordance with the permissions that the user has.
